mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month change in the federal funds rate directly alters the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows; negative changes reduce the cost of capital and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative FEDFUNDS changes do not produce significantly positive returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and rising unemployment creates a nonlinear negative wealth effect that amplifies equity risk premiums beyond the sum of individual effects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth negative and UNRATE increasing
failure_condition: In joint occurrences of RSAFS decline and UNRATE increase, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than during periods with only one condition present (interaction t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals manufacturing capacity normalization and inventory rebuilding, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings momentum surprises to the upside.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following months where INDPRO accelerated during identified post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth concurrent with elevated retail sales indicates demand outstripping productive capacity, prompting margin compression fears and input cost spikes that reduce future earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 1.5pp and RSAFS level > trailing 12-month median
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of occurrences when both INDPRO and RSAFS are jointly elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in new residential construction reflect improving household formation and credit availability, which precede durable goods consumption and positive wealth effects, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following 3-month rising HOUST are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent represents restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions, suppresses equity risk appetite, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55 percent of instances when the CPI acceleration trigger is met.
